main_functions.description: Handles creation of a new user account with required fields, validation, and inline error messages. Covers field format validation, password rules, confirmation matching, duplicate email detection, and mandatory field checks.

sub_functions.sub_function_name: Field Format and Composition Validation (Email and Password)
sub_functions.description: Validates email format and password composition rules, including length and character requirements. Inline error messages are verified for invalid inputs.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that the system rejects invalid email formats (e.g., missing @, missing domain, invalid characters) and accepts valid ones based on standard email format (local-part@domain) until exact rule is clarified. For invalid formats, verify that an inline error message is displayed near the email field and is meaningful.

Verify that passwords meeting length (8-12 characters) and composition (at least one number, one special character, one alphabet character) requirements are accepted, and those violating any rule (e.g., length 7 or 13, missing number, missing special char, missing alphabet) are rejected with an inline error message near the password field. Include boundary values (7, 8, 12, 13) to ensure edge cases are covered.

sub_functions.description: Ensures that the password and confirm password fields match during registration, with inline error messages for mismatches.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that an error is raised and displayed inline when password and confirm password do not match, and that submission proceeds when they match.

sub_functions.description: Validates the format of phone number and username fields. Since exact formats are undefined, tests rely on assumed reasonable patterns and inline error messages.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that invalid phone numbers (e.g., non-numeric, too short, too long) are rejected with an inline error message, and valid ones are accepted based on assumed pattern.
Verify that invalid usernames (e.g., containing special characters, too short, too long) are rejected with an inline error message, and valid alphanumeric ones are accepted based on assumed pattern.

sub_functions.description: Ensures that an account cannot be created with an email that is already registered, and an inline error is shown.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that attempting to register with an already existing email returns an appropriate error (e.g., 409 conflict) and an inline error message is displayed near the email field.

sub_functions.description: Checks that all mandatory fields (first name, last name, username, phone number, email, password, confirm password) are provided; empty submissions are rejected with inline error messages.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that submitting the registration form with any or all required fields empty results in appropriate inline error messages for each missing field.

main_functions.description: Manages the email verification process required before first login and activates the account upon successful verification. Covers pre-login blocking, account activation, verification link processing (expiration, resend, token validity, reuse prevention).

sub_functions.description: Enforces that email verification is completed before allowing the first login attempt.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that a user with an unverified email cannot log in, and an appropriate error message is displayed (e.g., 'Please verify your email before logging in').

sub_functions.description: Confirms that the account status becomes active only after successful email verification.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that after completing email verification, the account is marked as active and the user can log in successfully.

sub_functions.description: Handles verification link validity, expiration, resend functionality, and token lifecycle. Assumptions include a reasonable expiration time (e.g., 24 hours) and ability to resend.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that an expired verification link does not activate the account and displays an appropriate error message (e.g., 'Link expired, request a new one').
Verify that a user can request a new verification email and the new link is valid for activation, and any error messages are meaningful.
Verify that using a malformed or non-existent token fails with an appropriate error message.

sub_functions.description: Ensures that once a verification link has been used successfully, it cannot be reused; also handles verification attempts on an already active account.

test_categories.test_intent: Verify that a verification token that has already been used to activate an account cannot be used again, and an appropriate error message is displayed.

test_categories.category_name: Verification Attempt on Already Active Account
test_categories.test_intent: Verify that attempting to verify an email for an account that is already active (e.g., via a link or resend) results in an appropriate error message.
